novel_features.name: Multi-venue watchlist sweep
novel_features.description: Fan out across a venue ID file or list and return one merged, deduplicated, date-sorted event list — the watchlist primitive behind any curated 'what's on at my venues' workflow.
novel_features.rationale: Discovery API has no multi-venue endpoint; pulling per-venue and merging requires a local store and dedup logic.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user has a curated list of venues they care about and wants one merged feed; replaces hand-rolled per-venue fan-out scripts.

novel_features.name: Residency collapse
novel_features.description: Collapse runs of same-name + same-venue events into one row per residency with first_date, last_date, night_count, and id_list — so a 16-night opera season shows as one entry, not 16.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-row GROUP BY over the local events table; the API returns every performance independently with no dedup signal.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When listing upcoming events would otherwise show many near-duplicate rows for Broadway tours, opera seasons, or comedy residencies.

novel_features.name: Attraction tour view with on-sale flag
novel_features.description: For a given attraction (artist/team/touring show), return every upcoming event sorted by date, with city, venue, on-sale status, and a flag for events going on-sale within 7 days.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local events × attractions and parses dates.status.code + sales.public.startDateTime per event; no single API call returns this view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When tracking an artist across cities or watching for presale windows.

novel_features.name: On-sale-soon scanner
novel_features.description: Local query for events whose public on-sale falls in the next N days, sorted ascending — the canonical 'presale watch' view that no API endpoint provides.
novel_features.rationale: Requires filtering on sales.public.startDateTime against the current time, which is only feasible after the events are in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user wants to be alerted to upcoming on-sale dates without polling each artist manually.

novel_features.name: Classification bucketing
novel_features.description: Local join of events × classifications, grouped by segment and genre, with event count and three example events per leaf — the bucketed view newsletter authors and local-scene trackers reach for.
novel_features.rationale: Requires GROUP BY across local events and classifications tables; the API returns one classification per event but offers no aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When summarizing 'what's on this month' broken down by music vs theatre vs comedy vs sports.

novel_features.name: Named persistent watchlists
novel_features.description: Save, list, run, and remove named filter sets (venue IDs, attraction IDs, segments, DMA IDs) that persist across runs in the local SQLite store — the generic primitive any curated 'my venues' workflow composes from.
novel_features.rationale: Watchlists are stored locally in a new SQLite table; the API has no concept of named filter sets.
novel_features.example: ticketmaster-pp-cli events watchlist save seattle --venue-ids KovZ917Ahkk,KovZpZAFkvEA,KovZpZA1klkA
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the same curated venue/artist/genre filter recurs across many queries.

novel_features.name: Stream-shaped dedup
novel_features.description: Read an event JSON array from stdin or the local store, apply a deduplication strategy (name+venue+date, or tour-leg), and write the deduped stream to stdout — composes with any upstream command.
novel_features.rationale: Mechanical filter that works on any event JSON; agent-friendly because it's stateless and pipeable.
novel_features.example: ticketmaster-pp-cli events list --keyword phish --json | ticketmaster-pp-cli events dedup --strategy tour-leg
novel_features.why_it_matters: When merging results from multiple queries or sources and the duplicates need to be removed before agent processing.

novel_features.name: Markdown what's-on brief
novel_features.description: Render a markdown 'what's on' report grouped by night → venue → events with classification labels and price bands, suitable for newsletter, Obsidian, iMessage, or agent context.
novel_features.rationale: Local query plus mechanical group-by + markdown formatting; no API endpoint produces formatted reports.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user needs a paste-ready event summary for a chat thread, newsletter, or LLM context.

novel_features.name: Price-band distribution
novel_features.description: Bucket events by priceRanges.min into <$50 / $50-100 / $100-200 / $200+ bands and report count + sample events per band, grouped by classification.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local SQL aggregation over priceRanges; the API does not aggregate price data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user wants to know where the affordable shows are this month, or how a venue's pricing skews.

narrative.headline: Every Discovery v2 endpoint plus offline search, multi-venue watchlists, residency dedup, and on-sale tracking no API call exposes.
narrative.value_prop: ticketmaster-pp-cli is the first single-binary CLI for the Ticketmaster Discovery API. It absorbs the full read-only surface (events, venues, attractions, classifications, suggest) and adds a local SQLite store with FTS search, named watchlists, residency collapse, tour-view with on-sale flags, and markdown briefs — the workflows real users built scripts to handle.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authentication is a single Ticketmaster Discovery API consumer key, passed as the `apikey` query parameter on every request. Register at https://developer-acct.ticketmaster.com and copy the Consumer Key from your My Apps dashboard. Set TICKETMASTER_API_KEY in your shell environment. The free tier allows 5000 requests/day at 5/second.

quickstart.comment: Verifies the API key is set, the Discovery API is reachable, and the local store is initialized.
Sync the API surface into the local SQLite store for offline search and aggregation.
Fan out across multiple venues and return one merged event list.
Collapse Broadway/opera residencies into single rows with first/last date and night count.
Render this week's events as a paste-ready markdown brief.

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm TICKETMASTER_API_KEY matches the Consumer Key (not Consumer Secret) at developer-acct.ticketmaster.com/products-and-docs/user/me. Re-source your shell.
Free tier is 5000 req/day, 5/sec. Run `ticketmaster-pp-cli doctor` to see today's call count, or upgrade the plan.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results for a venue you know exists
troubleshoots.fix: Venue may not be Ticketmaster-primary; try a DMA query with classification filter instead. Use `suggest <venue-name>` to discover the canonical venue ID.
Resale and dynamic-priced events often omit priceRanges; this is upstream behavior, not a CLI bug.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when a user asks 'what's on at <venue/metro>', 'where is <artist> playing', or 'what concerts this weekend'. Best for repeat queries against curated venue/artist watchlists (offline FTS shines here), residency-heavy venues (opera, Broadway, comedy), and agent contexts where compact JSON output keeps token usage low. Skip when checkout/purchase is needed — that requires the Commerce API, which this CLI does not cover.

recipes.title: Seattle watchlist composition (no Seattle-specific code in the CLI)
recipes.explanation: Save a named watchlist of Seattle venue IDs. Then run "events watchlist run seattle --days 60 --json" to apply the filter across upcoming events; pipe through "events dedup --strategy name-venue-date" for the final cleaned feed. Replaces a 595-line bash script.

recipes.title: Track an artist across all upcoming dates with on-sale flags
recipes.explanation: Returns every upcoming tour stop with city, venue, and a flag for stops going on-sale within a week. The --select narrows the deeply-nested Discovery payload to just what an agent needs.

recipes.title: Weekend brief for a metro
recipes.explanation: Render a markdown brief of the next 3 days of music events in Seattle-Tacoma (DMA 383) - paste-ready for Obsidian or an iMessage thread.

recipes.title: On-sale watch for rock shows
recipes.explanation: Two-week-out scan for rock events going on public sale; pipe to an alerting script.
